title: daucus humilis
intro: Daucus humilis, also known as wild carrot, is a perennial plant in the parsley family (Apiaceae) that resembles the common carrot but lacks edible roots. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Daucus humilis include relief from digestive issues, such as indigestion and diarrhea, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory problems. Daucus humilis can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, with the root being used for its medicinal properties in various herbal preparations.
